items.title: Flights unaffected after fire at Mumbai's international airport [WATCH]
items.summary: HYDERABAD: Flight operations were unaffected after a fire broke out at Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (CSMIA) on Thursday evening. Airport operations remain unaffected," a spokesperson said. A fire broke out at Terminal 1 of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport in Mumbai this evening, reportedly caused by an electrical issue.

items.title: Senior cop who orchestrated heists targeting expatriates was attached to Bukit Aman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A suspended senior police officer who masterminded a series of armed robberies around Kepong was last attached to the federal police headquarters. City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus told the New Straits Times that the suspect, a Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP), served in Bukit Aman's Commercial Crime Investigation Department. The suspects, aged 31 to 51, impersonated police officers before entering the victims' homes.

items.title: Siblings injured in late-night crash in Paya Terubong
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Two siblings were injured after being hit by a car while attempting to cross the road along Jalan Paya Terubong here on Tuesday night. Northeast district police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said the incident occurred at about 11.10pm near SJK(C) Aik Hua. Lee said police received a traffic report on the incident at about 12.05pm the following day.

items.title: #NSTviral: Viral video captures intoxicated passenger vandalising Grab car
items.summary: The clip, shared by Socmed UpdateInfo , shows the man refusing to exit a black Grab vehicle upon reaching his destination. Moments later, the situation escalated when the man began kicking and punching the vehicle. The video ends with the man still behaving aggressively near the vehicle.

items.title: A Quiet Conversation Done Well
items.summary: APPROACHING your boss to discuss a sensitive matter is rarely comfortable, yet it is often necessary. Care, preparation, and emotional intelligence are essential in ensuring that the discussion remains constructive rather than confrontational. Vague concerns tend to lead to vague outcomes.

items.title: Air raid sirens across Israel after rocket launches from Lebanon
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Air raid alerts rang out across Israel early Friday, including in the commercial hub of Tel Aviv and in the southern coastal city of Ashdod following rocket fire from Lebanon. Israel and Hizbollah exchanged fire repeatedly on Thursday. The Israeli army's Home Front Command issued alerts for several areas following Friday's rocket fire, including the Tel Aviv area and southern communities far from the Lebanon border.

items.title: Iran's supreme leader says does not seek war in written message
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in his latest written message the Islamic republic did not want war with the United States and Israel, but would protect its rights as a nation, state television reported Thursday. "We did not seek war and we do not want it," he said in the message read out on state TV, weeks after his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed on February 28, the first day of the war. Iran this week agreed to a fragile two-week ceasefire with the United States that could lead to peace negotiations after threats of annihilation from US President Donald Trump.

items.title: Ministry to ramp up checks, action on illegal care homes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tighter enforcement, enhanced monitoring and stricter action against offenders are among measures to be rolled out following allegations of sexual abuse at an unregistered care home in Gombak. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the ministry, through the Social Welfare Department, was committed to strengthening enforcement under the Care Centres Act 1993 (Act 506) to minimise risks of negligence, neglect and abuse. "For unregistered premises, surprise enforcement checks will be carried out based on complaints, as well as surveillance in areas suspected of housing illegal care centres," she told the New Straits Times .

items.title: Account 3 withdrawals mostly by lower-income contributors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Contributors earning below RM3,000 per month make up the majority of withdrawals from the Employees Provident Fund (EPF) Flexible Account at 51.1 per cent. But they only make up 28.6 per cent of the total withdrawal value. Malay members remained the largest group to make withdrawals at 64.1 per cent, making up 57.8 per cent of the total withdrawal value.

items.title: Ecuador raises tariffs on Colombia to 100 per cent from 50 per cent
items.summary: QUITO/BOGOTA: Ecuador's government on Thursday said it was raising tariffs on imports from its larger neighbour Colombia to 100 per cent from a prior level of 50 per cent, citing Colombia's alleged failure to implement border security measures. "After confirming Colombia's failure to implement concrete and effective border security measures, Ecuador is compelled to take sovereign action," the government said in a statement. Ecuador said the tariffs would take effect from May 1.

items.title: EPF Flexible Account a lifeline for many
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Withdrawals from the Employees Provident Fund's (EPF) Flexible Account are commonly used for daily household needs, reflecting the rising cost of living. The EPF's latest research report on "Enduring Today, Shaping Tomorrow: Akaun Fleksibel as a Financial Lifeline for Wellbeing" showed that 93 per cent used their withdrawn savings to cover daily necessities, followed by emergency-related expenses (81.7 per cent). "In addition, nearly three-quarters of members (74.4 per cent) used the funds for debt and investment obligations, while 73.3 per cent directed them towards seasonal and lifestyle expenses," the report said.

items.title: When money feels tight, how will Malaysians vote for brands with their wallet?
items.summary: Research firm Rakuten Insight’s latest survey this month shows that Malaysians would not necessarily go for what is cheapest, and that they also want a good quality product when choosing a brand. “53.4 per cent cite lowest price as a driver. Buying the wrong cheap thing is an expensive mistake,” he added ‘Buatan Malaysia’ something Malaysians want in a financial squeeze In Rakuten Insight Malaysia’s Malaysia Cost Pressure Pulse (MCPP) April survey, 1,052 Malaysian adults were asked to choose what they consider when deciding on a brand, with the top five factors being: lowest price (53 per cent); promotions/ cashback (39 per cent); durable, long-term value (27 per cent); trusted quality (won’t risk wasting money) (26 per cent); and preference for local-made (18 per cent).

items.title: Shipping toll for Hormuz passage sharply divides nations
items.summary: PARIS: Iran's imposition of a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz looks set to be a key point of contention at talks opening in Pakistan Friday. When the United States and Israel launched their attack on Iran on Feb 28, Iran responded by blocking passage through the key strait to all but a handful of vessels. US President Donald Trump's position, however, appears more ambivalent.

items.title: Ukraine and Russia will ceasefire for Orthodox Easter
items.summary: MOSCOW: Ukraine and Russia will ceasefire for the Orthodox Easter holidays, the warring countries' leaders said on Thursday, announcing a rare 32-hour halt in fighting. The four-year war previously saw limited and short truces, but Moscow and Kyiv were quick to trade accusations of violations. With talks on ending the four-year-old conflict derailed by the Middle East war, Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy said this week that he had made a holiday truce proposal through the US.

items.title: Melania Trump denies any Epstein connection, seeks end to 'lies'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: First lady Melania Trump on Thursday denied having any relationship with disgraced financier and sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, reviving a highly sensitive issue that her husband has repeatedly sought to downplay. She denied online speculation that Epstein had introduced her to Donald Trump, saying she met her husband at a New York City party in 1998. "I am not Epstein's victim," she said.

items.title: Former Iranian foreign minister dies from attack wounds
items.summary: TEHERAN: A former Iranian foreign minister, Kamal Kharazi, died Thursday from wounds suffered in US-Israeli strikes on Apr 1, Iranian media reported. His wife was killed in the strike on their home in Teheran, media reported. Kharazi was Iran's envoy at the United Nations in New York and then became foreign minister from 1997 to 2005, under reformist president Mohammad Khatami.

items.title: Ready to rumble? Hunter Biden challenges Trump's sons to cage match
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Americans could see a first-of-its-kind fight as they prepare to celebrate the country's 250th independence: A cage match between the sons of a sitting US president, and the son of a former president. The improbable possibility arose on Thursday when Hunter Biden, son of former Democratic president Joe Biden, challenged Republican president Donald Trump's eldest sons, Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump, to a fight. Biden said he got a call from conservative social media commentator Andrew Callaghan, who told him that he was organising the match.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon set for ceasefire talks next week: US official
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Israel and Lebanon will hold talks next week in Washington, a State Department official said Thursday, as concerns grow that fighting involving Hizbollah could cause the fragile US-Iran truce to unravel. Israel's prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu had earlier on Thursday ordered his ministers to seek direct talks with Lebanon, pushing for Iran-backed Hizbollah's disarmament. Later Thursday, Israel's army said it was striking Hizbollah launch sites in Lebanon.

items.title: Trump warns Iran against tolls for Hormuz shipping
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Thursday warned Iran against imposing a toll for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, after Teheran agreed to reopen the crucial route as part of a two-week ceasefire. Trump said on his Truth Social platform. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Expand carpool services, Loke urges e-hailing firms
items.summary: Its minister, Anthony Loke, said the approach would allow passengers to share rides more efficiently at more affordable fares, while maintaining a balance between industry needs and consumer welfare. Loke said the Transport Ministry was boosting efforts to sustain the aviation industry through discussions with Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) and the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM). We will continue engaging the aviation industry to outline measures to support its sustainability," said Loke.

items.title: 'Preserve heritage or lose it'
items.summary: Failure to preserve historical buildings could result in the permanent loss of Selangor's heritage in the capital, said Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. He said without conservation efforts, invaluable historical assets could vanish over time. On the cost of restoration, Sultan Sharafuddin said such investments were necessary to safeguard national heritage.

items.title: Big Caring unveils automated Klang distribution centre and HQ
items.summary: KLANG: Big Caring Group has launched a new distribution centre in Klang, alongside a centralised corporate headquarters, to strengthen its supply chain through technology innovation. The facility handles over 90,000 order lines daily, reflecting growing scale across its operations. Big Caring executive director Lim Sin Yin said the automated distribution centre was built from Covid-19 lessons, where demand volatility and supply chain strain exposed the limits of traditional models, and efficiency alone was insufficient.

items.title: Philippines unveils Spratly coast guard command
items.summary: The Philippine Coast Guard yesterday unveiled its first dedicated command centre in the Spratly island chain, a flashpoint in the disputed South China Sea that has been the site of repeated confrontations with Chinese vessels. The headquarters of a newly established coast guard district, formerly overseen from neighbouring Palawan, will cover an area of about 68,000 sq km. An AFP journalist travelling to the new command centre on Pagasa Island, known as Thitu in the Philippines, saw multiple Chinese Coast Guard vessels patrolling nearby waters.

items.title: War in the Middle East: Latest developments
items.summary: PARIS, France: The latest developments in the Middle East war: - Israel seeks Lebanon talks - Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he had ordered his cabinet to open direct talks with Lebanon to disarm Iran-backed militant group Hizbollah and establish "peace relations" between the two countries. "Negotiations will focus on disarming Hizbollah and establishing peace relations between Israel and Lebanon." - Netanyahu threatens Hizbollah - Earlier, Netanyahu said Israel would keep hitting Hizbollah "wherever necessary."

items.title: Pakistan hails 'restraint', slams Lebanon strikes ahead of US-Iran talks
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan praised "restraint" in the Middle East war on Thursday, but condemned ongoing Israeli attacks on Lebanon as Islamabad prepared to host expected talks between Iran and the United States. Iran has said Lebanon was a key part of the ceasefire and argues Israeli attacks breach the truce. Iran's deputy foreign minister, Saeed Khatibzadeh, said "Iran was on the verge of responding to the ceasefire violation last night."

items.title: Israeli campaign in Lebanon 'could cause peace process to fail': Merz
items.summary: BERLIN: The continued Israeli military campaign in Lebanon could jeopardise peace talks expected between the United States and Iran over the war in the Middle East, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Thursday. "We view the situation in southern Lebanon with particular concern," Merz said. President Donald Trump has claimed victory in the Middle East war after agreeing a two-week truce to allow talks between US and Iranian negotiators.

items.title: New year, new wings: 15th Boeing 737-8 lands at KLIA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines Bhd has received its 15th Boeing 737-8, marking the airline's first aircraft arrival of the year, which touched down at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) last night. The carrier said the arrival of the new aircraft represented another milestone in its ongoing fleet expansion. The aircraft was flown home by Captain Reza Abdul Rahim, Captain Megat Mohd Yasser Mohd Yusoff, Captain Mohamad Shazreen Sazali, First Officer Meor Muhamad Arif Meor Mohd Mahazir and First Officer Aidid Azizul Hashim, whose dedication ensured a safe and smooth arrival in Malaysian skies, the airline said.

items.title: Malaysia-Thailand railway extension project to strengthen bilateral trade
items.summary: PATTANI: The proposed railway extension from Rantau Panjang in Pasir Mas, Kelantan to Sungai Golok in Narathiwat, Thailand, is expected to enhance the transport sector and boost bilateral trade between the two countries. Malaysian Ambassador to Thailand Datuk Wan Zaidi Wan Abdullah said the project, currently under review by the Malaysian government, could strengthen supply chains and facilitate the movement of goods. He said that Southern Thailand's strategic location, bordering Malaysia, continued to support cross-border exchanges in trade, tourism, education, and social relations.

items.title: Believers rejoice as Jerusalem's holy sites re-open
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Just before 5am at Jerusalem's Al-Aqsa Mosque compound, a Muslim worshipper prayed in tears. A few hours later, similar emotion overcame Christians and Jews as the city's holy sites reopened following the truce with Iran. For the first time in 41 days, Muslim worshippers returned to Al-Aqsa Mosque, Jews to the Western Wall and Christians to the Church of the Holy Sepulchre.

items.title: WFH policy to maintain productivity without hurting economy – Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The upcoming implementation of the work-from-home (WFH) policy for civil servants, set to begin next Wednesday, will not disrupt economic activity, but is aimed at maintaining productivity, says Economy Minister Datuk Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. "Working from home does not mean hindering economic activity. Addressing public concerns over potential panic buying, he said the government had taken early measures by reviewing supply issues following the activation of the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) last month.

items.title: Singapore, Malaysia strengthen defence ties, close cooperation
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Singapore and Malaysia have reaffirmed their long-standing defence ties, emphasising the importance of close cooperation in maintaining regional stability. In a statement on Thursday, Singapore's Ministry of Defence (Mindef) said the commitment was highlighted during a meeting between Minister for Defence Chan Chun Sing and Malaysian Chief of Defence Force General Datuk Malek Razak Sulaiman. "General Malek Razak's visit underscores the warm and enduring bilateral defence relations between Singapore and Malaysia.

items.title: Five-day remand for fifth suspect in Kelantan ketum bribery scandal involving cops
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 9 — Another suspect has been remanded for five days until Monday for allegedly soliciting and accepting about RM12,000 in bribes in exchange for not taking legal action against an individual found carrying ketum leaves in his car. Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid issued the remand order against the man in his 30s following an application by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) at the Kota Bharu Magistrate’s Court here today. Meanwhile, Kelantan MACC director Azmin Yusoff confirmed the arrest, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 16(a)(B) of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Malaysia orders halts to Malbatt patrols, seeks UN review as Lebanon mission grows unsafe
items.summary: REMBAU, April 9 — Malaysia will request the United Nations (UN) to review the terms of deployment for peacekeeping missions, including the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 under the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL), following the worsening security situation in the country. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said this was due to the current situation in Lebanon no longer being conducive for a peacekeeping mission, raising concerns over the safety of more than 500 Malbatt personnel stationed there. “The situation in Lebanon is no longer aligned with a peacekeeping mission.

items.title: Temporary fuel hiccups at some Shell stations; company assures customers of steady supply
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — Shell Malaysia is working to restore fuel supply at its petrol stations nationwide amid reports that some places have run out of fuel. The oil-and-gas giant said it is focusing on maintaining steady supply across its retail network following last week’s diesel price hike. Petrol station operators have noted challenges balancing working capital for fuel sales and replenishment amid rising unsubsidised fuel prices, while subsidised prices like RM1.99 for RON95 remain.

items.title: Teraju’s new fund aims to turn Sabah start-ups into scale-up stars
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 9 — The Bumiputera Agenda Steering Unit (Teraju) has introduced the Bumiputera Sabah and Sarawak Scale-Up Fund (BSSF) as a strategic driver to accelerate the growth and scaling of Bumiputera companies in Sabah. Teraju chief executive officer Junady Nawawi said the fund provides flexible financing support and guarantee mechanisms to improve access to capital and help local companies grow, particularly those in high-value growth sectors. “In the context of Sabah, this value creation must align with the Sabah Maju Jaya (SMJ) 2.0 agenda led by Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor,” he said in his speech at the Teraju Aspirasi Outreach Sabah 2026 event here today.

items.title: No shortcuts to sovereignty: DPM Fadillah stresses urgent energy, water reforms amid West Asia tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — Recent geopolitical tensions in West Asia have emphasised the urgency for Malaysia to strengthen its energy security and accelerate its transition efforts, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof today. He said the volatility of global energy markets proved that energy security is not theoretical, but is deeply structural. “We face a delicate balance, transitioning away from fossil fuels while safeguarding energy security.

items.title: Johor homestay murder case: Ex-workshop worker loses appeal against conviction, 30-year jail and caning sentence
items.summary: A three-judge panel led by Datuk Azman Abdullah, in a unanimous decision, dismissed the appeal by Lu Kian Pin, 41, to set aside the conviction and sentence handed down by the Johor Bahru High Court. Delivering the judgment, Mohd Radzi said the court found that the appellant (Lu) had failed to show that the High Court had erred in convicting him on the charge. “There is no reason for this court to interfere with the findings made by the High Court.

items.title: Lights, camera, funding: Putrajaya pumps RM300m to spotlight local talent and reel in global productions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today announced new measures to support Malaysia’s film industry, including a major funding allocation aimed at attracting international productions and promoting local content. “This allocation will help draw more international productions to Malaysia and simultaneously create greater job opportunities for local industry players,” Fahmi told reporters at the Seniman Symposium 2026 here. In addition to Fimi, he also said that the government has launched Dana Citra, a RM10 million fund designed to support the development of content rooted in Malaysia’s national identity.

items.title: Kelantan police chief: Officers fired in self-defence during UiTM Machang machete attack, attempted murder probe under way
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 9 — A man with multiple criminal records was injured after being shot when he attempted to attack police officers with a machete in front of Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) Machang last night. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said police had earlier received information about a shooting at the location at 11.17pm. He said the officers were forced to fire several shots at the suspect in self-defence and to prevent serious injury.

items.title: Ministers: Govt backs Malaysia’s film industry with insolvency support, offers free mediation for disputes under RM250,000
items.summary: Also speaking at the same event, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said free mediation services will be offered for contract disputes valued below RM250,000. The initiative, she said, reflects the reality that most contracts, particularly within the creative industry, fall within that threshold. This, Azalina said, enables parties to resolve disputes without bearing high legal fees.

items.title: Malaysia, Brunei to empower disaster management commitment via Nadma-NDMC cooperation, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 9 — Malaysia and Brunei will continue to strengthen bilateral cooperation in disaster management, particularly expertise sharing, capacity building and joint training, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Ahmad Zahid who is also the Central Disaster Management Committee (JPBP) chairman, said the effort was implemented through collaboration between the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) and Brunei’s National Disaster Management Centre (NDMC), which has been the main platform for coordinating various initiatives since 2022. He said Malaysia and Brunei also agreed to emphasise the importance of implementing integrated cross-border disaster management exercises regularly as the two countries share a long land border.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: ‘Bangun KL’ initiative aims to reduce peak-hour traffic through earlier commutes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The “ Bangun KL: Ke Arah Kuala Lumpur Yang Lebih Sejahtera ” initiative aims to encourage people to enter the capital earlier, thereby easing peak-hour congestion, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories), Hannah Yeoh. She said the initiative focuses on behavioural change through incentives and cross-sector collaboration to address traffic challenges more holistically. That is why we introduced Bangun KL .

items.title: Malaysia’s rare-earth crown: Lynas plans heavy-element takeover to rival China
items.summary: GEBENG, April 9 — Australian mining firm Lynas told AFP on Wednesday it plans to expand its portfolio of rare earths extracted in Malaysia, as it hopes to bolster its position as a key alternative supplier to China. “The first step was to demonstrate that we are able to extract heavy rares (earths),” Lynas chief operating officer Pol Le Roux said in an interview at the company’s facility in eastern Malaysia’s Gebeng. Lynas Malaysia chief operating officer Pol Le Roux (centre) and Lynas Malaysia chief executive officer Amanda Lacaze (right) speak at the Australian mining firm’s Malaysian rare earths refinery in Gebeng, Pahang on April 8, 2026.

items.title: Fisheries Dept: Stockpiles can sustain Malaysia’s fish supply for three months if needed
items.summary: IPOH, April 9 — The country’s fish supply remains unaffected by the global energy crisis stemming from the West Asia conflict, the Department of Fisheries (DOF) director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said. He said fishing vessel operations in zones A, B and C are continuing normally, supported by government fuel subsidies. Adnan said aquaculture could step in to keep fish supplies steady if sea fishing faces disruptions, even as feed costs are set to rise due to logistics snags, as farmers’ existing stocks will maintain stable output.

items.title: MCMC denies blocking report, to take legal action against blogger Papagomo over false claims
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 9 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) will initiate legal action against blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, over false claims that the commission blocked a news report involving Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. The news report in question, published by the Malaysia Gazette portal, pertained to a court case involving the minister and Wan Muhammad Azri. “In this regard, MCMC will take appropriate legal action against Wan Azri concerning the dissemination of these false claims,” the statement read.

items.title: Johor KPDN busts diesel syndicate using modified lorries, seizes 3,300 litres worth RM73,296
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 9 — The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) Johor branch busted a diesel misappropriation syndicate, seizing 3,300 litres worth RM73,296 in an operation at the Sri Purnama Industrial Area today. She said inspections found that the modified lorry was fitted with three Intermediate Bulk Container (IBC) tanks containing liquid suspected to be diesel, amounting to 3,000 litres. “In addition, a tanker lorry was also found to contain liquid suspected to be diesel, estimated at 300 litres, bringing the total seizure to approximately 3,300 litres,” she said in a statement today.

items.title: Bomba rescues government surveyor injured by fallen tree in Sarawak forest
items.summary: LAWAS, April 9 — A government surveyor was injured after being struck by a fallen tree in the forest at Kampung Murud Suang here today. Lawas Fire and Rescue Station (Bomba) chief Clarence D. Primus Tiandun said the 37-year-old victim from Kampung Berawan, Limbang was found semi-conscious under the fallen tree at around 12pm. “The team, together with Health Ministry personnel, provided initial treatment to the victim at the scene.

items.title: DBKL: Jalan Yew bridge to close nightly from April 10–21 for reconstruction works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The route along the Jalan Yew bridge will be temporarily closed to all vehicles from 10pm to 5am from April 10 to 21. The closure involves the stretch between TRX and Bulatan Pudu, as well as parts of Jalan Cochrane and Jalan Pasar (from the Masjid Alam Shah junction to the Petron station on Jalan Pasar). “During this period, road users are advised to use alternative routes via Jalan Pudu, Jalan Chan Sow Lin and Bulatan Kampung Pandan to reduce congestion and ensure smoother traffic flow.

items.title: Kelantan woman fined RM2,000 for storing pornographic videos on phone
items.summary: BACHOK, April 9 — A woman was fined RM2,000 by the Magistrates’ Court here today after pleading guilty to a charge of storing about 100 pornographic materials including pornographic videos in her cellphone last week. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Aiman Syahmi Yazid prosecuted while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation lawyer, Norizaidah Abdul Salam. The accused paid the fine set by the court.

items.title: Najib settles defamation suit with Dzulkefly over nepotism claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The 2022 defamation suit filed by Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad against former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak over a Facebook post alleging nepotism involving the health minister’s daughter’s appointment to a government-linked trust body was resolved in the High Court here today. Najib’s counsel, Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah, informed Judge Mahazan Mat Taib that the matter had been resolved, with his client having prepared a statement following discussions with Dzulkefly. Najib then began reading his statement.

items.title: Deputy works minister: 30 federal projects in Borneo states expected to be completed in 2026 (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUCHING, April 9 — An estimated 30 development projects under the federal government in Sabah and Sarawak are expected to be completed within this year, said Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan. Ahmad said the target follows the completion of 21 projects in 2025 worth RM466 million, of which 12 projects valued at RM203 million were in Sabah and nine projects worth RM263 million were in Sarawak. “Of these, 54 projects are located in Sarawak and 68 in Sabah,” he said.

items.title: Kuching man fined RM3,000 for hurling petrol bomb at house in intimidation bid
items.summary: KUCHING, April 9 — A 23-year-old man was fined RM3,000 in default 10 months’ jail by the Magistrates’ Court here for threatening another man by hurling a petrol bomb at the latter’s house. Sulaiman and another man, who is still at large, committed the offence at a house in Kampung Demak Baru Phase 2, Jalan Bako at 5.35am on March 21. Upon investigation, Sulaiman was arrested around 1.50pm on April 1 at a house in Kampung Tupong Tengah, Petra Jaya.

items.title: Penampang eatery owner fined RM20,000 for stealing electricity
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 9 — An eatery operator was fined RM20,000 in default six months’ jail by the Sessions Court here for dishonestly using electricity last year by bypassing the cut-out fuse linked to the premises’ fuse box. Andrew Mali, 58, pleaded guilty before Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan to a charge under Sub-Section 61(3)(c) of the Electricity Supply Enactment 2024, which is punishable under Sub-Section 61(3)(ii) of the same Enactment. The charge stated that Andrew committed the offence which caused Sabah Electricity Sdn Bhd (SESB) to charge him less for electricity or caused SESB to suffer a loss.

items.title: Trial resumes in June for teacher accused of causing alarm in Zara Qairina case
items.summary: SEPANG, April 9 — The Magistrates’ Court here today set June 11 to 12 as the date for the continuation of the trial of a school English teacher who was charged with causing public alarm by claiming that Zara Qairina Mahathir was put in a washing machine. Magistrate Suriani Ujang set the date for the first day of trial proceedings involving Siti Hajar Aflah Sharuddin, 40, with several more witnesses expected to appear to give their statements. The police officer also informed the court that he lodged a police report at the Kota Kinabalu International Airport Police Station, Sabah on August 6, 2025 after seeing two video recording posts via TikTok accounts related to the death of Zara Qairina which is currently under investigation.

items.title: Hajiji urges Sabah Bumiputera entrepreneurs to seize blue economy opportunities
items.summary: He said the blue economy will serve as a key driver under the Sabah Maju Jaya 2.0 (SMJ 2.0) roadmap, aimed at transforming the state’s economic structure towards high-value-added, inclusive and sustainable growth. Hajiji said the approach builds on Sabah’s maritime strengths and rich biodiversity, spanning strategic sectors including modern fisheries, aquaculture, marine biotechnology, maritime logistics and nature-based tourism. “During this period, Sabah recorded a historic milestone when state revenue nearly reached RM7 billion for the first time, with RM6.96 billion in 2022, RM6.97 billion in 2023, and remaining resilient at RM6.76 billion in 2025,” he said.

items.title: Suspended police corporal denies robbing woman at Kelantan grocery shop
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 9 — A police corporal suspended from duty pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to robbing a woman at a grocery shop in Kampung Kemumin last year Mohd Irfan Azieyadi Abdul Aziz, 45, entered the plea after the charge was read out to him before Judge Nik Habri Muhamad. He is accused of robbing Nik Zainab Nik Zul Azhar at Rohan Trading, Lot 1869, Jalan Sabak, Pengkalan Chepa, at about 10.30pm on November 7, 2025. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Kamarul Hasyime Rosli proposed bail of RM15,000 with one surety, along with a condition requiring the accused to report monthly to the Kota Bharu District Police Headquarters (IPD).

items.title: Police tracking suspect over ‘Allah’ graffiti at temple in Melaka
items.summary: JASIN, April 9 — Police are tracking down a suspect believed to be involved in a case of graffiti involving the words ‘Allah’ and ‘Bismillahirrahmanirrahim’ at a Chinese temple in Pantai Siring here. Jasin district police chief Supt Lee Robert said initial information received from residents indicated that they had previously seen a man believed to be mentally unstable scribbling the words ‘Allah’ on walls and electricity poles. Checks on the land ownership status will also be carried out through the Jasin District and Land Office.

items.title: Caretaker pleads guilty to 15 abuse charges involving boys at unregistered children’s home in Batu Caves
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — A caretaker at an unregistered children’s home pleaded guilty in two Sessions Courts in Ampang today to 15 counts of physical and non-physical sexual assault, as well as acts against the order of nature involving five boys. According to 12 of the charges, the man was accused of committing physical and non-physical sexual assault against five boys aged between eight and 13 at a welfare home run by an organisation in Batu Caves between November 2025 and March 27, 2026. For the offence of physical sexual assault, the charges were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, which carries a maximum sentence of 20 years’ imprisonment and is also liable to whipping upon conviction.

items.title: High Court to decide on Shamsul Iskandar’s bid to transfer graft case on April 28
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — The High Court today fixed April 28 to deliver its decision on the application by former senior political secretary to the Prime Minister, Datuk Seri Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin, to transfer the five corruption charges made against him from the Sessions Court to the High Court here. Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin set the date after hearing submissions from Shamsul Iskandar’s counsel, Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, and deputy public prosecutor (DPP) Farah Ezlin Yusop Khan. “I would suggest April 28, 2026, (for decision),” she said.

items.title: Here’s why Malaysians will cut out cafes, eating out first, but not food deliveries in economic pinch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 9 — When prices go up, will you stop ordering food deliveries first or stop eating outside at restaurants first? “When Malaysians have to choose between sacrificing the restaurant or the delivery app, they are consistently choosing to sacrifice the restaurant,” the research data arm of Rakuten Group said about its Malaysia Cost Pressure Pulse’s (MCPP) April 6 to April 8 survey results. The survey showed that those who would reduce spending on meals outside increased from 52.9 per cent in March to 61.6 per cent in April (which is the category with the largest jump with 8.7 percentage points), while the food delivery and quick commerce category only had a 1.5 percentage point increase (30 per cent in March will cut this out first, 32 per cent in April).
